title: 4 week view in Calendar view
excerpt:
Description
A 4 week view for the calendar widget would be awesome. For us it is very important to see the upcoming week(s) in a quick overview due to the use of digital signage. When arriving at the last week of the mon…

posts.author: safeships (Stein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

A 4 week view for the calendar widget would be awesome. For us it is very important to see the upcoming week(s) in a quick overview due to the use of digital signage. When arriving at the last week of the month, we unfortunately cannot see the upcoming week as it is in the next month. Current week + next 3 weeks are important for our planning!

What are you trying to achieve

4 week view in the Monday calendar widget instead of ‘year, month, week, day’ only

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Stein,

Voted for your feature request

If you’re open to marketplace apps we can add a 4-week view to Calendar Plus. It would open to this week and the 3 following weeks, and look something like below:

Let me know and we can add it pretty quickly.

–

posts.author: safeships (Stein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Reinier,

Thank you for your reply. We do not mind using Calendar Plus and have actually looked into this. If you can implement this into the Pro (flat rate) plan, we will definetly use this as it would completely solve our problem!

Thank you in advance and please keep us updated if possible.

Kind regards,

Stein.

posts.author: safeships (Stein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Reinier,

Any update on this matter?

Thank you in advance.

Kind regards,

Stein.

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Stein,

Sorry about that! I didn’t receive a notification about your replies. I’ve received your support request too and we’ll get something going for you

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
For everyone following this thread, the 4-week view is now available in Calendar Plus for everyone!

–
